items.headline: CAR-T cell therapies going in vivo
items.preview: A shift towards in vivo CAR-T therapies is gaining momentum, attracting major investments.
items.article:
Ex vivo CAR-T therapies have saved the lives of tens of thousands of cancer patients who had no other option left. While they remain a promising approach, there is now a swing towards in vivo CAR-T cell therapies. This swing has seen cash flow into the space, and big pharmas have sealed major deals over the past year.
Why this matters: Lilly's recent $7 billion acquisition of Kelonia Therapeutics highlights the growing interest in in vivo CAR-T therapies, which could simplify treatment and improve patient access. The lead program KLN-1010 has shown promising early trial results, achieving a 100% early measurable residual disease-negative response.
Lilly's venture into the in vivo CAR-T space is fairly recent, having announced it would purchase Orna Therapeutics in February. The Massachusetts-based startup created a circular RNA platform, which is the base of its pipeline. The pipeline is led by ORN-252, which is in phase 1/2 trials to treat B cell-driven autoimmune diseases and cancers.

items.headline: AAV2 Capsid Clearance and Neuronal Trafficking Dynamics
items.preview: New insights into AAV2 capsid behavior could enhance gene therapy for CNS disorders.
items.article:
In a groundbreaking study published in Gene Therapy, researchers have unveiled intricate details about the behavior of adeno-associated virus serotype 2 (AAV2) capsids within the central nervous system (CNS). This research sheds critical light on the mechanisms underpinning viral vector clearance and neuronal trafficking, phenomena central to the safety and efficacy of gene therapy applications targeting neurodegenerative diseases.
Why this matters: Understanding capsid trafficking and clearance at this granular level informs the development of next-generation viral vectors. The study reveals that capsid ubiquitination plays a pivotal role in targeting AAV2 for degradation, which could be manipulated to improve transgene expression duration.
The implications of this study are vast, as it sets the stage for clinical trials that incorporate these insights, potentially enhancing treatment options for patients with diseases such as Parkinson’s and Huntington’s.

items.headline: RBM14 Boosts Prostate Cancer by Enhancing Glycolysis
items.preview: RBM14's role in stabilizing HK2 mRNA reveals new targets for prostate cancer therapy.
items.article:
In a groundbreaking advancement that could redefine the therapeutic landscape of prostate cancer, researchers have unveiled a novel molecular mechanism by which prostate cancer metastasizes. The study highlights the pivotal role of RBM14 in facilitating the aggressive spread of prostate cancer cells through the stabilization of HK2 mRNA, catalyzing a metabolic shift that ramps up glycolysis.
Why this matters: This research integrates RNA biology, metabolism, and epigenetics into a comprehensive framework explaining prostate cancer metastasis. The findings suggest that targeting RBM14 or its downstream pathways could provide transformative benefits in treating metastatic prostate cancer.
The study also explored small molecule inhibitors capable of disrupting the RBM14-HK2 interaction, revealing preliminary efficacy in curtailing metastatic spread.

items.headline: New genome editing method could swap entire genes and correct 1000 mutations at once
items.preview: A novel genome editing technique allows for large DNA segment insertions, enhancing gene therapy.
items.article:
New technology enables the insertion of a large segment of DNA into a genome, potentially expanding gene therapy treatment from cancellation of disease-causing mutations to replacement of an entire gene. Researchers describe a prime assembly approach that avoids a bottleneck in gene therapy by eliminating the need for double-strand breaks.
Why this matters: This method allows for the correction of up to 1,000 heterogeneous mutations at once, significantly enhancing the potential for gene therapy applications. The technique can insert DNA segments containing up to 11,000 base pairs, far exceeding previous methods.
The team plans to further explore the best delivery vehicles for this technique and test its effectiveness in vivo.

items.headline: Translational repression of viral RNAs supports persistent arbovirus infection in mosquitoes
items.preview: Research reveals how arboviruses maintain persistent infections in mosquito vectors.
items.article:
Arboviruses induce acute lytic infection in human cells but establish persistent infection in their mosquito vectors, a strategy essential for sustained viral transmission. This study investigates how mosquito cells maintain continuous production of viral progeny without compromising host cell viability.
Why this matters: Understanding the mechanisms that support persistent infection in mosquitoes could lead to novel vector control strategies. The research indicates that persistent infection is characterized by reduced viral protein production due to translational repression of viral RNAs, suggesting a balanced host-virus translational state.
This mechanism may represent a general feature of arbovirus persistence in mosquito cells, with implications for controlling viral transmission.
